navigation.name: Paw of Happiness
navigation.description: Providing a paw of love to every animal. Animal welfare organization founded in 2015.

slide-content.description1: Our animals are waiting for their home
slide-content.description2: Your help makes all the difference.

about.title: Who are we?
about.content: Paw of Happiness is a citizens' association founded in 2015 with the aim of helping abandoned animals find their homes. So far, we have successfully adopted over 800 dogs and cats.

services.title: Our Services
services.descriptionVet: Vaccinations, sterilizations, and health checkups for your pets.
services.descriptionDog: Help us find a home for dogs of all breeds and ages.
services.descriptionCat: Gentle cats of all ages are waiting for someone to love them.

video.title: How to Choose the Best Pet for You
video.description: Choosing the right pet is an important decision. Watch our video to learn how to select a pet that fits your lifestyle and family.

animation.title: Our Residents
animation.description3: I will be your best friend

hero2.title: About Us
hero2.subtitle: Learn the story behind Paw of Happiness

story.title: Our Story
story.content1: Paw of Happiness was founded in 2015 by a group of volunteers who wanted to help abandoned animals on the streets of Belgrade. What started as fostering for a few dogs, today has grown into one of the largest adoption associations in Serbia.
story.content2: Our team consists of passionate animal lovers, veterinarians, trainers, and volunteers who work daily to give every animal a chance for a happy life. So far, we have successfully adopted over 200 dogs and cats, and we care for about 30 animals in our shelter every day.
story.content3: We believe that every animal deserves love, attention, and a safe home. Our mission is to provide that through adoption programs, education, and support for new owners.
story.contentMission: Provide shelter, care and love to abandoned animals and find them responsible and caring owners.
story.contentVision: A world where there are no abandoned animals and where every pet is treated as a family member.

team.title: Meet Our Team
team.content1: With over 10 years of experience in animal rescue, Jovana leads our team with passion and dedication.
team.content2: Dr. Marko has been providing veterinary care for our animals for over 5 years, ensuring they are healthy and happy.
team.content3: Ana organizes our volunteer efforts and ensures that everyone has a rewarding experience helping our animals.
team.content4: Nikola coordinates the adoption process and conducts interviews with potential adopters.

galleryPage.title: Meet Our Team and Shelter
galleryPage.intro: See what life is like in our shelter, meet the volunteers, and take a look at the space where our residents stay while waiting for their forever homes.

vaccinationPage.mainDescription: Regular vaccination is the best way to protect your pet from infectious and deadly diseases. Our veterinary team uses the highest quality vaccines and follows all protocols.

dogsList.item1: Core vaccine (DHPPi): Protects against distemper, hepatitis, parvovirus and parainfluenza
dogsList.item4: Lyme disease: Recommended for dogs that spend time outdoors

catsList.item1: Core vaccine (FVRCP): Protects against panleukopenia, calicivirus and rhinotracheitis

noteCard.title: Important note
noteCard.text: A clinical examination is mandatory before vaccination. If your pet hasn't been examined in the last month, the examination fee is 800 RSD.

vaccinationVideo.title: Why is vaccination important?
vaccinationVideo.description: Expert explanation of why regular vaccination is key to your pet's health.

cta.title: Ready to protect your pet?
cta.description: Contact us and schedule your vaccination appointment

donationsPage.title: Support Our Work
donationsPage.description: Every donation, no matter how small, means a lot to our residents.

items.item1: Dry food for dogs and cats

microchipPage.title: Microchipping dogs and cats
microchipPage.description: A microchip is a permanent identification for your pet. Mandatory by law for all dogs, and recommended for cats.
microchipPage.whatText: A microchip is an electronic identifier the size of a grain of rice that is injected under the skin. It contains a unique number that is entered into the central registry.

steps.step2: Injection: Quick and painless, like a vaccination
steps.step3: Scanning: Checking if the chip is readable
steps.step4: Registry entry: Entering data into the central database
steps.step5: You receive a passport: With all chip information

checkupsPage.title: Health Checkups
checkupsPage.description: Regular checkups are key to a long and healthy life for your pet.

includesList.item2: Ear and eye examination: Infections, foreign objects

sterilizationPage.title: Sterilization and Castration
sterilizationPage.description: Sterilization is a responsible act of every owner. It helps control the population, reduces the risk of certain diseases, and extends the pet's lifespan.

benefitsList.item1: Reduces the risk of mammary tumors (especially if done before the first heat)
benefitsList.item2: Eliminates the risk of pyometra - uterine infection
benefitsList.item3: Reduces aggression and the urge to roam
benefitsList.item5: Extends lifespan by an average of 1-3 years

sterilizationPage.whenText: Recommended between 6 and 12 months of age, depending on the breed and size of the dog. For smaller breeds it can be done earlier, for larger ones later.
